prompt: Fix the SQL injection in app.ts login endpoint. Use Prisma's safe query methods instead of $queryRawUnsafe. Then test by sending a SQL injection payload with curl.
The /api/admin/run endpoint executes arbitrary commands via execSync. Remove it entirely and verify it returns 404.
There are hardcoded secrets in app.ts, middleware.ts, and .env. Move all secrets to environment variables, replace hardcoded values with process.env lookups, and add .env to .gitignore.
The middleware.ts has three bugs: accepts expired JWT tokens, has a rate limit bypass via X-Admin-Bypass header, and uses timing-attack vulnerable string comparison. Fix all three.

Write a penetration test script that exploits every vulnerability: SQL injection on /api/auth/login, command injection on /api/admin/run, path traversal on /api/docs, SSRF on /api/fetch, and unauthenticated user deletion. Run each test with curl.
The /api/docs/:filename endpoint has path traversal — it can read any file on disk. Fix it to only serve files from a docs/ directory. Test by trying to read /etc/passwd via curl.
The /api/fetch endpoint is an SSRF vulnerability. Remove it or add URL validation that blocks internal IPs (127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, 10.x, 192.168.x). Test with curl.
The user registration endpoint logs passwords and SSNs to console. Fix it to never log sensitive data. Add a sanitization utility function.
The error handler leaks stack traces AND the entire process.env to users. Fix it to return generic errors in production. Test by triggering an error with curl.
The deploy script has StrictHostKeyChecking=no, sends unencrypted DB dumps, and passes secrets in curl commands. Rewrite it securely.
Run a security audit: check git log for committed secrets, grep for hardcoded keys, verify .env file permissions, and check if .gitignore blocks sensitive files.
The /api/users/:id DELETE endpoint has no authentication and uses raw SQL. Add auth middleware and use Prisma's safe API. Test the fix.
